feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business One
feed.subtitle: SAP Business One Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Sales Order to AR Invoice query
entry.content:
i want result as document level in one line. but in my query it shows itemwise. please help.

 

SELECT
   
    T0.DocEntry,
    
CASE
   WHEN T2.DocDate IS NOT NULL
   THEN CAST(YEAR(T2.DocDate) AS VARCHAR(4)) + '-' +
        RIGHT(CAST(YEAR(T2.DocDate)+1 AS VARCHAR(4)),2)
   ELSE ''
END AS [Invoice FY],
 
    T4.SeriesName,
    T0.DocNum AS [SO No],
    T0.DocDate AS [SO Date],
    T0.CardCode as [Customer Code],
    T0.CardName as [Customer Name],
 
CASE T0.U_Type   
WHEN 1 THEN 'Sales'
WHEN 2 THEN 'Service'
WHEN 3 THEN 'Blank'
WHEN 4 THEN 'Adv-PO-Sales'
WHEN 5 THEN 'Adv-PO-Service'
WHEN 6 THEN 'Sales - Life Sciences'
WHEN 7 THEN 'Service - Life Sciences'
WHEN 8 THEN 'Sales - FD'
WHEN 9 THEN 'Service - FD'
WHEN 10 THEN 'Sales - MSF'
WHEN 11 THEN 'Service - MSF'
END AS'Type',
 
    T0.U_Region as [Region],
    Sum((T1.[Price]*T1.Quantity)) as 'SO Total',
 
CASE 
WHEN T0.DocStatus = 'O' AND T0.CANCELED = 'N'
    THEN Sum((T1.[Price]*T1.Quantity)) ELSE 0 END AS [Open SO Amount],
 
CASE 
WHEN T0.DocStatus = 'C' AND T0.CANCELED = 'N'
    THEN Sum((T1.[Price]*T1.Quantity)) ELSE 0 END AS [Closed SO Amount],
 
CASE 
WHEN T0.CANCELED = 'Y'
    THEN Sum((T1.[Price]*T1.Quantity)) ELSE 0 END AS [Cancelled SO Amount],
 
--CASE    
--     WHEN T0.CANCELED = 'Y' THEN 'Cancelled'    
--     WHEN T0.DocStatus = 'C' THEN 'Closed'    
--     WHEN T0.DocStatus = 'O' THEN 'Open'    
--ELSE 'Unknown'END AS [SO Status],
 
    T2.DocNum AS [Invoice No],
 
--CASE 
-- WHEN T2.CANCELED = 'N'
--    THEN Sum((T3.[Price]*T3.Quantity))
--END As 'Inv Total'
 
    T2.DocDate AS [Invoice Date],
Sum((T3.[Price]*T3.Quantity)) as 'INV Total'
 
--CASE    
--     WHEN T2.CANCELED = 'Y' THEN 'Cancelled'    
--     WHEN T2.DocStatus = 'C' THEN 'Closed'    
--     WHEN T2.DocStatus = 'O' THEN 'Open'    
--ELSE 'Unknown'END AS [Invoice Status]
 
 
 
FROM ORDR T0
INNER JOIN RDR1 T1 ON T0.DocEntry = T1.DocEntry
LEFT OUTER  JOIN dln1 T3A ON T1.DocEntry = T3A.BaseEntry AND T1.LineNum  = T3A.BaseLine AND T3A.ItemCode=T1.ItemCode
LEFT OUTER  JOIN INV1 T3  ON T3."BaseEntry" = (case when T3."BaseType"='17' then T1.DocEntry when T3."BaseType"='15' then T3A.DocEntry else 0 end)  
AND T3."BaseLine" = (case when T3."BaseType"='17' then T1.LineNum else T3A.LineNum end) 
AND T3."ItemCode" = (case when T3."BaseType"='17' then T1.ItemCode else T3A.ItemCode end) 
LEFT OUTER JOIN OINV T2 ON T3.DocEntry = T2.DocEntry
LEFT outer  JOIN NNM1 T4 ON T0.Series = T4.Series
LEFT JOIN RDN1 T5    ON T5.BaseType = 15   AND T5.BaseEntry = T3A.DocEntry   AND T5.BaseLine = T3A.LineNum
 
Where 
--T0.DocDate BETWEEN [%0] AND [%1] and (T2.docDate IS NULL OR T2.DocDate <= [%1])
T0.DocEntry = '71380'
and T5.DocEntry IS NULL
--and T0.CANCELED = 'Y'
--and (T2.CANCELED = 'N')
 
Group By T1.Price,
T1.Quantity,
T3.[Price],T3.Quantity,
T1.DiscPrcnt,
T0.DocNum,    
T0.DocDate,    
T2.DocNum,    
T2.DocDate,    
T0.CANCELED,    
T2.CANCELED,    
T0.DocStatus,    
T2.DocStatus,    
T0.CardCode,
T0.CardName,
T0.U_Type,
T0.U_Region,
T0.DocEntry,
T4.SeriesName
 
 
ORDER BY 
    T0.DocDate,
    T0.DocNum,
    T0.CardCode

entry.title: Suggestion Required for Managing Enquiry Process
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We would like your suggestion regarding the enquiry process in SAP Business One.

Currently, for every enquiry, a Lead or Customer Code needs to be created. However, many enquiries do not result in confirmed orders, which leads to the creation of a large number of unnecessary Lead/Customer Codes in the system.

Our requirement is to manage enquiries without creating a Lead or Customer Code initially. Once the customer confirms the order, we would like to enter the customer details and create the Customer Code at the order booking stage.

Could you please suggest the best practice or standard process in SAP Business One to handle this requirement? If there is any recommended approach to avoid creating unnecessary customer codes while still managing enquiries effectively, please let us know.

Looking forward to your guidance.

Thanks & Regards,

entry.title: How to extract multi references from XML Bank Statements
entry.content:
Hi community,

I'm looking for a way to extract multiple invoice numbers from an unstructured text to use for payment matching.
In SAP Note https://me.sap.com/notes/3055456 it mentions "When you have imported a bank statement with multiple reference numbers, that is, multiple numbers with commas in between in the <Ustrd> field of the bank file, those numbers will now be separated according to the commas as multiple rows in the Multiple Payments window."

My challenge is how to extract the reference numbers and bring the information in the comma separated format!?
We receive often single payments for various invoices.
Do you have a best practise? I only manage to extract single reference numbers with regular expressions.

Background is the switch from MT940 format to camt053 XML. My hope was that more structured camt053 will make use of official payment reference fields, but reality is that the information is still hidden in unstructured text in Ustrd field. It contains dates, values and invoice numbers.

How can I extract all found invoice numbers? Is there some kind of loop possibility or recursion in Electronic File Manager?

For example

RmtInf>
<Ustrd>/INV/631240 20.4.2026/INV/631263 21.4.2026</Ustrd>
</RmtInf>
should extract to 631240, 631263

Thanks very much

entry.title: PurchaseRequests API - RequriedDate Field with Typo (Requried instead of Required)
entry.content:
I'm reporting an issue encountered when creating Purchase Requests through the Service Layer API (OData v2) in SAP Business One.

 

NOTES:

  • The field is present when reading documents with GET (RequriedDate)
  • When creating new documents via POST, you must use exactly "RequriedDate"
  • This typo affects both the header and each line (DocumentLines)
  • Official SAP documentation doesn't mention this field, which complicates discovery
  • Error message references wrong table (OINV instead of PurchaseRequest-related tables)

QUESTIONS:

  • Is this a known typo in SAP B1 Service Layer?
  • Is there a list of known field name typos in the API?
  • Is there a plan to correct this field name in future versions?

VERSION TESTED:
SAP Business One 10.0 (approximately)
Service Layer API v2

 

ISSUE:
The required date field in the PurchaseRequests document has a typo in its name: it's called "RequriedDate" instead of "RequiredDate".

This caused confusion when attempting to create purchase requests, as:
- ReqDate ❌ Does not work
- RequiredDate ❌ Does not work
- ShipDate ❌ Does not work
- RequriedDate ✅ WORKS (correct field but with typo)

COCRRECT STRUCTURE FOR CREATING PurchaseRequests:

{
"DocDate": "2026-07-06",
"DocDueDate": "2026-07-06",
"RequriedDate": "2026-07-13",
"Comments": "Purchase request description",
"DocumentLines": [
{
"ItemCode": "ITEM001",
"Quantity": 1.0,
"WarehouseCode": "01",
"RequriedDate": "2026-07-13"
}
]
}

entry.title: sap b1 workflow service 500 Internal Server Error
entry.content: My sap b1 version is 10.00.250. When I tried to open the workflow service management platform, it prompted me 500 server internal error. I checked the corresponding log and it prompted me Caused by: com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.core.exception.B1SDKNotAuthenticatedException: Error on processing bearer token: Parameter b1-WorkflowServers-1025-wfcc-adm not found, I tried to restart the workflow service, but it had no effect. I also tried re-adding the workflow service in the sld service, but it didn't work either. I want to try reinstalling the workflow service, but I can't reinstall it alone. When I uninstall sld and reinstall them, It also prompted me that the system landscape directory contains no compatible service units. May I ask how to solve it?

entry.title: Canceled Purchase Order Draft remains in Approval Decision Report with Pending status and cannot be
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We have encountered an issue with the Approval Process in SAP Business One 10.0 for SAP HANA FP2508.

System Information

  • SAP Business One 10.0 for SAP HANA
  • Version: FP2508 (64-bit)

Issue Description

A Purchase Order Draft was submitted for approval and later manually canceled by the user.

However, after the cancellation:

  • The draft is still displayed in the Approval Decision Report.
  • The document Status is shown as Canceled.
  • The approval Answer remains Pending.
  • The draft cannot be removed from the Document Draft Report.

The system displays the following error message:

Cannot remove drafts as Draft No. 740 is in approval processes. Select only regular drafts for removal.

Database Verification

We verified the approval records and found that the approval workflow is still in Waiting status.

OWDD

  • DraftEntry = 740
  • Status = W (Waiting)

WDD1

  • User = FIN_HEAD 
  • Status = W (Waiting)

Although the document has already been canceled, the approval workflow is still waiting for approval.

Expected Behavior

After a draft document is canceled, the approval workflow should no longer remain in Waiting status.

The canceled draft should not continue to appear as a pending approval item, and it should be possible to remove the draft from the Document Draft Report if applicable.

Actual Behavior

The canceled draft remains in the Approval Decision Report with Answer = Pending, and the system still considers it to be in the approval process, preventing the draft from being removed.

Attachments

  • Approval Decision Report showing:
    • Status = Canceled
    • Answer = Pending
  • Purchase Order Draft (Canceled)
  • Error message:
    • Cannot remove drafts as Draft No. 740 is in approval processes.
  • Query results from OWDD and WDD1
  • SAP Business One version information (FP2508)

Could you please confirm whether this is the expected standard behavior or a product issue? If this is expected behavior, please advise the recommended method to clear the approval workflow and remove the canceled draft.

Thank you.

entry.title: Uso del Interactive Analysis en SAP B1 (Excel Report e Interactive Analysis)
entry.content:
Buenas,

Solicito apoyo para lo siguiente: he diseñado recientemente en SAP Hana una serie de vistas de cálculo para consumirlas usando Excel Report. Sin embargo, necesito también utilizar el apartado de  Interactive Analysis, que por lo que tengo entendido, es un Excel desde el que puedo manipular los datos de forma interactiva, a diferencia del Excel Report. Sin embargo, incluso cuando intento consumir en el Interactive Analysis una vista de cálculo estándar de SAP, me ocurre o bien, que se me cierra el Excel sin más, o me indica un mensaje de error indicando que el origen de datos no es válido.

No sé si hay que activar algo más específico para poder usar esta herramienta concreta del Interactive Analysis.

 

Pd: Los Excel Report he podido diseñarlos sin problemas. El problema es sólo este segundo apartado de Interactive Analysis.

 

Gracias de antemano!!

entry.title: How to show a comparison of price lists or last prices with vendor names from a Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a specific business requirement in SAP Business One. When a user is creating a Purchase Order (PO) and fills in the Item No., they want to see a quick comparison of prices for that item from various suppliers directly from the document row.

Ideally, they want to be able to right-click on the 'Unit Price' column and see an option like "Show Price List" (or similar) which pops up a window showing:

Vendor/Supplier Names

The prices they offer (either from multiple Price Lists or from Last Purchase Prices history).

For now, our standard approach that we already setup uses 'Unit Price' click on the right side mouse column to open "Last Prices", but I prefer to trigger it directly from the 'Unit Price' too but when click on the right side mouse it will show 'Price List' option.

Is there a standard way to achieve this? If not, what is the best customization approach?

Thank you in advance for your insights!

Best Regards,
Ester

entry.title: Clarification on VAT Tax G/L Account Display in Journal Entry
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have observed that the VAT Tax G/L account is being displayed in the Journal Entry with a zero amount, while the actual VAT amount is posted to a different VAT Tax G/L account.

Could you please help us understand why this additional VAT Tax G/L account with a zero amount is being included in the Journal Entry? We would appreciate your clarification on whether this is standard SAP Business One behavior or if it is related to any configuration.

Your guidance on this matter would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

Best Regards,

entry.title: SAP B1 Permission Issues
entry.content:
I'm having a problem right now—when I access SAP's appointment management, no records are showing up.
I think the problem is because I deleted the orphaned records in the CDPM table.

Does SAP have a feature to fix the permissions or restore them to their default settings?

Thank you

entry.title: Crystal Reports - Multiple Parameters in SAP B1 Web Client
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Is there any limitation for multiple parameters in SAP Business One - Web Client? Just in case, we are running FP2602 (MSSQL).

I've created a report with two parameters: JobCode and CurrencyOption. In the Desktop client, the report runs smoothly prompting all parameters set, whereas in the Web client it does not show the Currency parameter (which was the last one to come in) and throws an error when running: "An error has occurred while generating the PDF file".

Inspecting the browser, I see that CurrencyOption is being passed as null (as expected - there's no prompt to enter it)

Is this a WC limitation or is there a different way to implement multiple parameters here?

I'd appreciate your thoughts.

Best regards
Felipe

entry.title: Banking Payments paid Invoices posting dates of Invoices Query
entry.content:
Hi All

Please guide me to join INV1 to get GL Account Number in this Query.

 

SELECT T0.DocNum, T0.JrnlMemo, T0.DocType, T0.Canceled, T0.DocDate, T0.CardCode, T0.DocCurr, T0.DocTotal, T0.DocTotalFC, T2.DocNum as 'Invoice Number', T2.DocDate as 'Invoice Date', T2.JrnlMemo, T2.DocTotal as 'Original Invoice Amount', T3.DocNum as 'Credit Note Number', T3.DocTotal as 'Original Credit Note Amount', T4.TransId as 'JE Transaction Number', T3.JrnlMemo,  T1.InvoiceId, T1.DocEntry, (Case when T1.InvType = '14' then (T1.SumApplied * -1) else T1.SumApplied end) as 'Sum Applied', T1.SumApplied, T1.AppliedFC, T1.InvType, (case when T1.InvType= '30' then 'JE' when T1.InvType = '13' then 'AR Inv' when T1.InvType = '14' then 'AR Inv' when T1.InvType = '24' then 'RC' else '' end) as 'DocType',  T1.DocLine FROM ORCT T0 INNER JOIN RCT2 T1 ON T0.DocEntry = T1.DocNum LEFT JOIN OINV T2 ON T2.DocEntry = T1.DocEntry and T1.InvType = T2.ObjType

LEFT JOIN ORIN T3 ON T3.DocEntry = T1.DocEntry and T1.InvType = T3.ObjType

LEFT JOIN OJDT T4 ON T4.TransId = T1.DocEntry and T1.InvType = T4.ObjType

WHERE T0.Canceled = 'N' and T0.DocDate between [%0] and [%1] and T2.DocDate <[%2]

 

 

Kedalene chong

entry.title: Bank Statement Processing – Documents Not Displayed in Posting Proposal for Uncleared Rows (CSV Impo
entry.content:
We are using the Bank Statement Processing functionality in SAP Business One to perform automatic bank reconciliation by importing bank statements in CSV format.

The bank statement is imported successfully without any errors. However, after the import, when we click Posting Proposal for Uncleared Rows, the corresponding SAP Business One documents (such as Incoming Payments, Outgoing Payments, or other relevant transactions) that should be available for reconciliation are not displayed.

As a result, we are unable to proceed with the automatic reconciliation process.

Could you please guide us on the following:

  • What are the prerequisites for documents to appear in the Posting Proposal window?
  • What matching criteria does SAP Business One use to identify and display documents for automatic reconciliation?
  • Are there any mandatory configuration settings or mapping requirements that need to be completed before using this functionality?
  • Are there any mandatory fields in the imported CSV file that are required for the posting proposal to identify the corresponding SAP Business One documents?

Please let us know if there are any additional settings or checks that we should perform to resolve this issue.

entry.title: SAP Business One Service Layer returns HTTP 500 code 299 when calling a parameterized
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am working with SAP Business One on SAP HANA and Service Layer.

I created and exposed a HANA calculation view. The view is correctly published in the SL metadata:

GET /b1s/v2/sml.svc/$metadata

I am calling the view using the following URL:

GET /b1s/v2/sml.svc/VIEWParameters(IP_FECHAFIN=2026-07-31,IP_FECHAINI=2026-07-01)/VIEW

However, Service Layer returns:

{
"error": {
"code": "299",
"details": [
{
"code": "",
"message": ""
}
],
"message": "Internal server error."
}
}

The HTTP status code is: 500 Internal Server Error

Tests already performed

  1. The Service Layer session is valid. Other Service Layer endpoints work correctly.
  2. Other custom SL views without parameters work correctly.
  3. The parameterized calculation view executes correctly directly in SAP HANA Studio using the same date range.
  4. The HANA user has the required permissions.
  5. The view is present in $metadata, including the parameter entity and navigation property.
  6. Both /b1s/v1/sml.svc and /b1s/v2/sml.svc were tested, with the same result.

Additional observation

The SAP Business One Analytics Platform administration page is also showing:

CPU: undefined
Memory: undefined
Disk: undefined
License Server: undefined
Status: 500

The System Logs section of the Analytics administration console displays: Internal Error

Questions

  1. Is the URL syntax correct for a SL calculation view with two Edm.Date parameters?
  2. Is there a known Service Layer issue with HANA calculation-view parameters of type DATE?
  3. Should the parameters be changed from DATE to NVARCHAR and converted with TO_DATE() inside the calculation view?
  4. Could the Analytics Platform status 500 affect parameterized Semantic Layer views?
  5. Which Service Layer or Analytics log contains the actual HANA exception behind Service Layer error code 299?
  6. Is there a known SAP Note or patch related to this behavior?

Thank you.

entry.title: Crystal Reports Designer Performance Issue on Private Cloud Compared to Local Server
entry.content:
Hi SAP Expert Community,

I would appreciate your guidance regarding a Crystal Reports Designer performance issue.

Environment

I have two environments:

1. Local On-Premises Server

  • SAP Business One for Microsoft SQL Server
  • Crystal Reports Designer installed
  • Report opens almost instantly (within approximately 1 second).
  • Dragging, moving, and resizing report fields is immediate and very smooth.

2. Private Cloud Environment

  • SAP Business One for Microsoft SQL Server
  • Separate Server:
    • Microsoft SQL Server + SAP SLD
  • Separate Terminal/RDP Server:
    • SAP Business One Client
    • SAP Crystal Reports Designer
  • Both servers are configured with 12 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, which is sufficient for our workload and follows SAP's recommended architecture.

Issue

On the Private Cloud environment:

  • Opening Crystal Reports Designer takes approximately 2–3 seconds.
  • Dragging or repositioning report fields has a noticeable delay of 1–2 seconds before the field moves.
  • Overall report designing is less responsive than the local server.

entry.title: UDF Attachment Path Migration After Upgrade
entry.content:
Hi SAP Expert Community,

I need your assistance regarding a database upgrade from SAP Business One 9.3 (Microsoft SQL Server) to SAP Business One 10 FP2505 (Microsoft SQL Server).

ItemDetails

Previous VersionSAP Business One 9.3 (MS SQL)
Current VersionSAP Business One 10 FP2505 (MS SQL)
Affected ModulesJournal Entry, Incoming Payments & Etc
Previous SolutionSince these modules did not support native attachments in SAP B1 9.3, an add-on was developed to store the document file path in a User-Defined Field (UDF).
Current SituationSAP Business One 10 FP2505 now supports native Attachments for these modules.
IssueHistorical records still contain attachment paths only in the UDF. The native Attachment button remains empty because the attachment information was not migrated during the database upgrade.

entry.title: RSP Instance Backup Failed – HANA Backup Size Limit Error
entry.content:
Dear SAP Expert Team,

I am facing an issue with SAP Business One Remote Support Platform (RSP) on SAP HANA. I would appreciate your guidance in resolving this issue.

ItemDetails

ProductSAP Business One Remote Support Platform (RSP)
DatabaseSAP HANA
IssueIndividual Full Database Backups are completing successfully, but the Instance Backup fails every time.
Error MessageHANA backup failed: ERROR: Backup size limit argument value is not numeric. (Screenshot attached)

Troubleshooting Performed

StepStatus
Cleared the Instance Backup .pid log file✅ Completed
Completely reinstalled RSP, including manual removal of the RSP database and creation of a new database✅ Completed
Reconfigured RSP, including all required database and Instance Backup settings✅ Completed
Verified that RSP and SLD Full Database Backups are working correctly✅ Completed
Instance Backup❌ Still Failing

entry.title: Required Assistance on SAP Business One MSSQL High Availability from Private Cloud to Azure
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

I require your guidance in designing a High Availability (HA) and Disaster Recovery (DR) architecture for SAP Business One on Microsoft SQL Server hosted in a Private Cloud, with Microsoft Azure as the DR site.

Current Environment

ComponentConfiguration

SAP Business OneVersion 10 FP2505 (Microsoft SQL Server)
Production EnvironmentPrivate Cloud
Database ServerDedicated Microsoft SQL Server
SAP SLD & License ServerDedicated Server
SAP Business One ClientDedicated RDP/Terminal Server
Disaster RecoveryMicrosoft Azure

Objective

We are looking for a SAP-supported, cost-effective, and highly available solution that provides:

  • High Availability (HA)
  • Disaster Recovery (DR)
  • Minimal Recovery Time Objective (RTO)
  • Minimal or Zero Recovery Point Objective (RPO)
  • Automatic or simplified failover
  • Low Azure infrastructure cost

Current Challenges

AreaConcern
Azure Site Recovery (ASR)During failover, Azure requires time to provision and start the replicated virtual machines, increasing recovery time.
SAP SLD & License ServerAfter ASR failover, the VM hardware key changes, resulting in SAP Business One license/SLD issues.
Dedicated Azure SLDMaintaining an always-running SLD server in Azure significantly increases operational cost.
SQL Server ReplicationAsynchronous replication may result in data loss during an unexpected disaster.
Cost OptimizationWe want to avoid maintaining multiple permanently running Azure VMs while ensuring business continuity.

entry.title: SQL Server Maintenance Best Practices for SAP Business One
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

I would appreciate your guidance regarding Microsoft SQL Server database maintenance for a production SAP Business One environment.

Environment

ComponentDetails

SAP Business OneMicrosoft SQL Server
EnvironmentProduction
Database SizeMultiple Company Databases
Maintenance ToolSAP Business One RSP and SQL Server Maintenance Jobs

Concern

We would like to implement regular SQL Server database maintenance to maintain optimal database performance and control database growth.

However, before implementing a maintenance plan, we would like to understand whether these activities could have any impact on a live SAP Business One production environment.

Planned Maintenance Activities

ActivityFrequency (Planned)
Transaction Log Backup / Log CleanupWeekly
Database Reindex (Index Rebuild/Reorganize)Weekly
Database Statistics UpdateWeekly
Database Shrink (Data & Log Files)Only if required
Transaction Log ShrinkOnly if required
Database Integrity Check (DBCC CHECKDB)Weekly/Monthly

entry.title: DI API Disconnecting with Error: You are not connected to a company
entry.content:
I Have a DI API Windwos Service that works fine for 3-5 days with no errors, and then i get the You are not connected to a company, error.

I haven't  found i solution for this.

I am Using .NET 10 with various various threads using a singleton DI API  Company object Instance, i don't know if calling the GetBusinessObject method is not thread safe, the same with Recordset.DoQuery and adding or update any objects or services.

here is a snippet of the singleton

    internal sealed class DIAPI : IDIAPI
    {
        public Company Company { get; private set; }

        private readonly string _path;
        private readonly ILogger<DIAPI> _logger;

        public DIAPI(ILogger<DIAPI> logger, string path)
        {
            _logger = logger;
            _path = path;
            Company = new Company();
            (bool conected, string error) = Connect(_path);
            if (!conected)
            {
                _logger.LogError("erro ao tentar conectar: {erro}", error);
            }
        }

        private (bool connected , string error) Connect(string path)
        {
            if (Company is not null && Company.Connected)
                return (true, "");

            // parametros de conexão
            Conf conf = Conf.GetConfiguration(path);
            if(conf.DBType is null)
                throw new Exception("Tipo de banco de dados não informado na configuração.");

            string erro = string.Empty;
            Company = new()
            {
                LicenseServer = conf.LicenceServer,
                CompanyDB = conf.CompanyDB,
                UserName = conf.UserName,
                Password = conf.Password,
                DbServerType = (BoDataServerTypes)conf.DBType, // SQL 2019 é 15
                SLDServer = conf.SLDServer,
                Server = conf.Server,
                DbUserName = conf.UserDB,
                DbPassword = conf.PassDB,
                language = BoSuppLangs.ln_Portuguese_Br,
                UseTrusted = false
            };

            if (Company.Connect() != 0)
            {
                erro = $"{Company.GetLastErrorCode()} | {Company.GetLastErrorDescription()}";
                return (false, erro);
            }

            return (true, "");
        }
}

here is an exeple of injecting the singleton in a class

public Email_Queue (ILogger<Email_Queue> logger, IDIAPI api) { _api = api; _logger = logger; }


and using like this for exemple inside the many threads.

 

Recordset recordset = _api.Company.GetBusinessObject(BoObjectTypes.BoRecordset);


 Thanks in advance.
